name: webhooks
context: Send messages to Slack channels using incoming webhooks
info: Incoming webhooks are the simplest way to send messages from external systems into Slack. A webhook URL maps to a specific channel — POST a JSON payload and the message appears. No bot token or OAuth required. Webhooks support plain text, mrkdwn formatting, and Block Kit layouts. They are ideal for notifications, alerts, CI/CD status updates, and monitoring integrations. For two-way interaction, use [[slack/bots]] instead. For programmatic control over multiple channels, use [[slack/messages]] with the Web API.

sections.title: Incoming webhook setup
sections.body: Enable incoming webhooks in your Slack app settings. Add a webhook to a workspace and select the target channel. Copy the URL (`https://hooks.slack.com/services/...`). Store as `SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL` environment variable. Each URL is bound to a single channel. See [[slack#authentication]] for app setup.

sections.title: Sending messages via webhook
sections.body: POST JSON to the webhook URL with `text` and optional `blocks`. Use native `fetch()` or the `@slack/webhook` package. The response is `ok` on success. Include `username` and `icon_emoji` to customize the sender appearance.

sections.title: Message formatting
sections.body: Webhooks support plain text, mrkdwn formatting, and full Block Kit layouts (see [[slack/block-kit]]). Use mrkdwn for bold, italic, links, and mentions. Use blocks for headers, dividers, sections with fields, and context elements. See [[slack/messages#formatting]] for the mrkdwn syntax reference.

sections.title: Best practices
sections.body: Rate limit: ~1 req/sec per webhook. Always include fallback `text`. Treat URLs as secrets — rotate if compromised. Handle errors by checking response body. For two-way communication, use [[slack/bots]]. For multi-channel posting, use [[slack/messages]] with the Web API.

text.description: Message text (required fallback even when using blocks)

blocks.description: Block Kit blocks for rich message layout

iconEmoji.description: Override the webhook's icon with an emoji (e.g., :robot_face:)

depends.reason: Requires a Slack app with incoming webhooks enabled
